channel.title: Digg AI Feed from X
channel.description: Top 10 AI stories from Digg's X-ranked tech feed with click-through links to original source content.

item.title: Trump administration adviser says he had to brief cabinet officials on dataset contamination and Roko's Basilisk
item.description: Other academics recalled explaining rationalist concepts like the Waluigi Effect to military officials
item.content:encoded:
Other academics recalled explaining rationalist concepts like the Waluigi Effect to military officials

Original source: https://x.com/sriramk/status/2073105386878460093/photo/1

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/mf4103mt

An outgoing tech adviser to the Trump administration described unusual translation duties that included walking cabinet officials through Roko's Basilisk and dataset contamination, highlighting how online rationalist thought experiments have surfaced in high-level policy settings. 46 580 34 121 54K SÓ | @S_OHEIGEARTAIGH JC | @JAMESCHAM JA | @JACHIAM0 EM | @EMOSTAQUE SK #143 | @SRIRAMK

  • Views: 59,152
  • Likes: 636
  • Bookmarks: 134
  • Quotes: 15
  • Replies: 33
  • Posts: 12

Top authors

  • Sriram Krishnan
  • Emad
  • Joshua Achiam
  • James Cham
  • Seán Ó hÉigeartaigh
I once tried to explain 'the Waluigi Effect' to a Rear Admiral. It did not go well for either of us.

item.title: Instructor creator Jason Liu asks developers why they still rely on ChatGPT over OpenAI Codex
item.description: Engineer Andrew Mayne highlighted ATLAS as his primary reasoning tool
item.content:encoded:
Engineer Andrew Mayne highlighted ATLAS as his primary reasoning tool

Original source: https://x.com/jxnlco/status/2073133239057961085

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/0wld49f4

Jason Liu, the Instructor library creator now working on OpenAI's Codex team, turned to developers with a direct question about lingering ChatGPT habits even as Codex handles more code-specific work. Early replies surfaced two concrete differences rather than vague preferences, highlighting where the tools still diverge in practice. 782 732 4 112 120.3K CL | @_CHENGLOU HH | @HAMELHUSAIN AM | @ANDREWMAYNE CA | @CHRISALBON JA | @JXNLCO BT #593 | @TUNGUZ

  • Views: 126,008
  • Likes: 745
  • Bookmarks: 114
  • Quotes: 22
  • Replies: 774
  • Posts: 7

Top authors

  • Bojan Tunguz
  • jason
  • Chris Albon
  • Andrew Mayne
  • Hamel Husain
Codex feels like ChatGPT Pro. That is probably the best way I could describe it.

item.title: Mistral AI releases Leanstral 1.5, a 119B MoE model for Lean 4 formal proof engineering
item.description: The open model achieved 100% on the miniF2F benchmark.
item.content:encoded:
The open model achieved 100% on the miniF2F benchmark.

Original source: https://mistral.ai/news/leanstral-1-5/

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/7qph702e

Mistral just dropped an open 119B-parameter MoE model tuned specifically for Lean 4 formal proof work, where only about 6B parameters stay active at once. It saturates the miniF2F benchmark at 100 percent while posting 587 solved problems on PutnamBench and fresh highs on the FATE-H and FATE-X sets. 138 1.6K 130 478 192.5K EL | @ELIEBAKOUCH B( | @BEFFJEZOS T( | @TEORTAXESTEX KA | @YACINEMTB AM #256 | @ARTHURMENSCH

  • Views: 46,375
  • Likes: 574
  • Bookmarks: 202
  • Quotes: 8
  • Replies: 27
  • Posts: 12

Top authors

  • Arthur Mensch
  • kache
  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • Beff (e/acc)
  • elie
very nice release by mistral (with a tech report!!), they replace the complicated scaffold of previous lean models by a simple coding agent and just scale test time compute budget with compaction every 200k it's also quite interesting that you don't really see the impact of compaction in this curve (or maybe this is why they see diminishing returns?) ~6B lean mid training token, SFT with 50% lean traces and then RL with CISPO

item.title: Industry speculation points to an upcoming Thursday launch for OpenAI's rumored GPT-5.6 Sol model
item.description: The launch would precede Google DeepMind's rumored Gemini 3.5 Pro.
item.content:encoded:
The launch would precede Google DeepMind's rumored Gemini 3.5 Pro.

Original source: https://x.com/bridgemindai/status/2073008280255774919

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/tyrwge2n

A cluster of social posts is buzzing about OpenAI potentially rolling out its GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra variant around July 9, yet the only firm event on record remains the limited preview of the broader GPT-5.6 family that began June 26 for roughly twenty trusted partners. 182 2.6K 80 171 179.7K AV | @ALTRYNE CH | @KIMMONISMUS AC | @ANDREWCURRAN_ B( #530 | @BEFFJEZOS

  • Views: 181,730
  • Likes: 2,640
  • Bookmarks: 172
  • Quotes: 9
  • Replies: 174
  • Posts: 6

Top authors

  • Beff (e/acc)
  • Andrew Curran
  • Chubby♨️
  • Alex Volkov @ AI Engineer
Sounds like Gemini 3.5 Pro arrives around July 17th with a brand-new pretrain. Unless, of course, the government blocks it. People are pretty down on Google right now, but I think they'll be surprised. July is a month for Kaiju fights.

item.title: Peter Thiel warns Vatican-backed global AI regulation efforts could undermine the US in its technology race with China
item.description: Thiel also predicted a democratic-socialist takeover of the Democratic Party.
item.content:encoded:
Thiel also predicted a democratic-socialist takeover of the Democratic Party.

Original source: https://edition.cnn.com/2026/07/02/us/peter-thiel-aspen-pope-china-ai-cec

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/i47o16du

At the Aspen Ideas Festival Peter Thiel argued that Pope Leo XIV's May encyclical calling for AI to be disarmed could slow American development while leaving Chinese programs untouched, effectively tilting the technology contest in Beijing's favor. 109 965 80 212 86.3K CH | @KIMMONISMUS RP | @ROHANPAUL_AI SZ #84 | @SUCHENZANG

  • Views: 86,613
  • Likes: 968
  • Bookmarks: 213
  • Quotes: 28
  • Replies: 81
  • Posts: 5

Top authors

  • Susan Zhang
  • Rohan Paul
  • Chubby♨️
https://edition.cnn.com/2026/07/02/us/peter-thiel-aspen-pope-china-ai-cec

item.title: Outgoing White House adviser Sriram Krishnan says Trump administration won't establish centralized FDA-style AI regulator
item.description: Krishnan's duties included briefing officials on DeepSeek and explaining Roko's Basilisk.
item.content:encoded:
Krishnan's duties included briefing officials on DeepSeek and explaining Roko's Basilisk.

Original source: https://ft.trib.al/iVU1cuP

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/pl5n7dbw

Outgoing White House AI adviser Sriram Krishnan told the Financial Times that the Trump administration plans to push back against heavy federal rules and any single centralized AI regulator, favoring instead a lighter touch that lets American companies move fast. 57 535 34 100 125.4K AC | @ANDREWCURRAN_ B( | @BEFFJEZOS T( | @TEORTAXESTEX DK #207 | @DAVIDSKRUEGER

  • Views: 23,026
  • Likes: 142
  • Bookmarks: 26
  • Quotes: 3
  • Replies: 9
  • Posts: 8

Top authors

  • David Krueger 🦥 ⏸️ ⏹️ ⏪
  • Teortaxes▶️ (DeepSeek 推特🐋铁粉 2023 – ∞)
  • Beff (e/acc)
  • Andrew Curran
you're welcome

item.title: Boom Supersonic founder Blake Scholl teases milestone post amid supportive reactions from tech investors
item.description: Jason Calacanis and Alexis Ohanian posted public congratulations.
item.content:encoded:
Jason Calacanis and Alexis Ohanian posted public congratulations.

Original source: https://x.com/bscholl/status/2073113580069892283/photo/1

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/37avg7ui

Boom Supersonic's CEO marked a regulatory turning point with a hangar photo and 'Mission accomplished' caption, timed right after the FAA floated rules that could finally permit civil supersonic flights over land if sonic booms stay below a strict noise threshold already shown by the company's XB-1 demonstrator. 76 3K 123 56 42.1K BS | @BSCHOLL AO | @ALEXISOHANIAN @J #416 | @JASON

  • Views: 42,714
  • Likes: 3,174
  • Bookmarks: 61
  • Quotes: 8
  • Replies: 66
  • Posts: 5

Top authors

  • @jason
  • Alexis Ohanian 🗽
  • Blake Scholl 🛫
Mission accomplished ✅

item.title: Chamath Palihapitiya says Tesla's reported $200 weekly AI spending cap suggests higher employee expenditures are likely wasteful
item.description: Commentators believe the limit sets a new industry budget benchmark.
item.content:encoded:
Commentators believe the limit sets a new industry budget benchmark.

Original source: https://x.com/Kalshi/status/2072771173767643139

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/gv14b0zo

Tesla's reported internal memo introduces a $200 weekly ceiling on third-party AI tool usage starting July 6, with manager approval needed to exceed it, and Chamath Palihapitiya publicly frames any higher outlay at the company as probable waste given its engineering caliber. 468 8.9K 336 1K 1.3M RP | @ROHANPAUL_AI CP #347 | @CHAMATH

  • Views: 1,282,001
  • Likes: 8,956
  • Bookmarks: 1,058
  • Quotes: 60
  • Replies: 410
  • Posts: 2

Top authors

  • Chamath Palihapitiya
  • Rohan Paul
The new AI budget benchmark for software engineers may have just landed at $800/month. This is Tesla’s new AI spending line. And given these engineers work near the limit of physical machines and code, so this should anchor software budgets everywhere.

item.title: AI Security Institute says agent benchmarks must report performance curves over compute rather than static scores
item.description: A 10x increase in agent time horizon requires far more tokens.
item.content:encoded:
A 10x increase in agent time horizon requires far more tokens.

Original source: https://safe.ai/blog/significant-increase-in-digital-labor-automation

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/de1juqfi

Static scores from fixed token budgets hide how much more agents could achieve with extra test-time compute. The AI Security Institute analysis shows performance often keeps climbing well beyond typical cutoffs, with some tasks only solved after tens of millions of tokens and newer models pulling further ahead as budgets grow. 14 130 10 28 15.1K TO | @TOBYORDOXFORD HB | @HERBIEBRADLEY IH | @SOUNDBOY AK #874 | @AMINKARBASI

  • Views: 15,151
  • Likes: 130
  • Bookmarks: 28
  • Quotes: 2
  • Replies: 12
  • Posts: 11

Top authors

  • Amin Karbasi
  • Ian Hogarth
  • Herbie Bradley
  • Toby Ord
Because it is a log-log chart and those slopes are all roughly linear, the relationship is roughly a power law. The 80% time horizon is rising as the 2/3 power of the number of tokens. This means every 10x of the tokens buys about 5x the time horizon.

item.title: AI consultant Hamel Husain calls traditional enterprise AI consulting and deployment practices an organizational anti-pattern
item.description: Practitioners say corporate bureaucracy frequently bottlenecks advanced AI system integration.
item.content:encoded:
Practitioners say corporate bureaucracy frequently bottlenecks advanced AI system integration.

Original source: https://x.com/xlr8harder/status/2073121026439786510

Digg story: https://digg.com/tech/qtm2toyv

Hamel Husain draws on more than a decade of hands-on AI work to argue that the familiar pattern of hiring outsiders to build and run enterprise AI systems tends to lock in the very problems it claims to solve, such as missing internal skills and slow decision processes, rather than building lasting capability inside the organization. 87 218 5 82 20.8K XL | @XLR8HARDER FB | @XEOPHON HH #1686 | @HAMELHUSAIN

  • Views: 21,557
  • Likes: 228
  • Bookmarks: 82
  • Quotes: 8
  • Replies: 81
  • Posts: 7

Top authors

  • Hamel Husain
  • Florian Brand
  • xlr8harder
The great AI paradox. Enterprises don't seem to have the brainpower to know how to use AI.
